evals.prompt: We have a growing family of reasoning skills. I need a top-level router package that stays discoverable, keeps child metadata explicit, and tells the agent what to do when the best child is missing.
evals.expected_output: Creates a router-focused package plan with a discoverable top-level SKILL.md, explicit child inventory, and install-or-fallback behavior instead of treating this as a normal leaf skill.

evals.prompt: Create a reusable skill that writes one clean problem statement from a messy user situation.
evals.expected_output: Routes away from create-router-skill because this is a leaf skill and should use create-skill instead.

evals.prompt: We have dozens of skills in flat folders and people keep getting confused. Should we just move them all into category directories or should we create family routers with child metadata?
evals.expected_output: Explains that the package should create a router when the real problem is discovery and child handoff, rather than blindly reorganizing folders as the only solution.
